name: Teleseminar Funnel
origin: Phone or audio-based seminar used as a teaching and selling mechanism — a recognized standalone revenue-generating selling format, distinct from video webinars. Single presenter (vs. the multi-speaker Telesummit).

whenToUse.summary: Use for a single-presenter audio/dial-in selling seminar — a Perfect-Webinar-style teach-then-pitch delivered by voice only (no slides), ideal for audio-native audiences or low-production setups.
goals: Sell an offer via a single-presenter audio teaching call
Reach an audio-first audience (commuters, coaching/consulting niches)
Run a webinar-style close without slides or video production
signals: Single host, no slides, audio-only delivery
Wants the Perfect Webinar close in audio form
antiSignals: Sub-$100 impulse buy (use VSL/5-Minute Perfect Webinar)

pageStructure.purpose: Capture the registration with a curiosity promise and the dial-in promise.
keyElements: 3 fascination bullets teasing the 3 secrets (taught by voice)

pageStructure.purpose: Deliver the dial-in / access details and maximize show-up.
Host the recorded call for those who missed it, within a window.
Present the order with the Stack and a deadline.

scripts: The call = Perfect Webinar delivered by voice (Intro/Hook → 3 Secrets → Stack → Close), no slides
Call open = Epiphany Bridge origin story
Registration headline + bullets + replay urgency = Copywriting Secrets
copyFramework.notes: It is the Perfect Webinar without visuals — the verbal storytelling and the verbal Stack must carry the whole close. Strong Epiphany Bridge delivery matters even more without slides.

automations: Attended vs no-show tag → replay sequence
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 builds the 4 pages with the dial-in delivery and audio replay; Skill 44 generates the opt-in form (email + phone), the audio player, the order form + bump, and the replay-expiry / cart-close countdowns.
